mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect immediate manufacturing sector strength and positive supply-side momentum, triggering short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during monetary easing periods, with a hit rate below 50% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5% YoY are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly translates to expanded corporate revenue bases and improved profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals immediate supply chain disruptions and manufacturing contraction, prompting rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops greater than 1.0% MoM are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in over 55% of cases during post-shock recovery periods.
